articles.title: Trump says King will be 'very safe' during US visit after security talks
articles.summary: The state visit will go ahead despite concerns raised after a gunman targeted an event attended by the president.

articles.title: Pakistan accused of attacking Afghan university
articles.summary: Air attacks on the province of Kunar have killed at least seven people and injured 75, sources tell the BBC.

articles.title: Gunmen raid Nigerian orphanage and kidnap children
articles.summary: The attack saw 23 children and the facility's proprietress kidnapped by gunmen on Sunday.

articles.title: Russian fighters confirm withdrawal from northern Mali city after separatist attacks
articles.summary: Ethnic Tuareg fighters say they are in control of Kidal following a weekend of nationwide attacks, alongside Islamist groups.

articles.title: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
articles.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

articles.title: Sri Lankan monks arrested after 110kg of cannabis discovered in their luggage
articles.summary: Officials say each monk was found with around 5kg of Kush - a particularly potent form of the drug - hidden within "false walls" in their luggage.

articles.title: At least 42 killed in Chad after water well dispute escalates
articles.summary: The dispute was initially between two families before escalating into a cycle of reprisal attacks.

articles.title: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
articles.summary: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

articles.title: Plane crash in South Sudan kills all 14 on board
articles.summary: The authorities are investigating but early reports suggest bad weather and poor visibility may be to blame.

articles.title: Michael Jackson biopic smashes box office record
articles.summary: The film received poor reviews but has stormed the box office, taking $217m (£160m) globally.
